item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Noting that I've cleared tags added erroneously. Readers, if you're not going to add all the tags for an article, it's best to just leave the page untagged and wait for an experienced tagger to take care of it. We have a team of people on the site who tag articles, and they search for pages that have no tags. Adding only a few tags prevents the team from finding the article, so if you're unfamiliar with the tags, it's totally fine to wait for them to take care of it.

Also, "phenomenon" is not a tag used by the SCP wiki. General rule of thumb, if you've added a tag and there's only one page that has that tag, that's not a tag you should be using.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't have much to say about this scp. I enjoyed it, +1. I wonder if we'll see more of this mysterious "boss" with power over the dead. The different perception each citizen has about Seraph could be a memetic hazard. Also, about
MTF-ζ "Public Eye", I think it should be something like: MTF ζ-number ("Public Eye"); at least that's what I always seen in other MTFs. Anyway, that last part is just me being nitpicky. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved it as a draft, love it as a skip! The highly motivated door-to-door salespeople for this niche local business, plus all the spooky corpse stuff, just teeters back and forth across the edges of comedy and squick in a way that I especially enjoy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This went from quirky to creepy to horrific in record time. I particularly noticed how quickly Gagnon was co-opted. This almost felt like a Factory SCP, but biological.

Some of those homes must be plentifully supplied with taxidermy by now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a cool and interesting skip that I enjoy.

(protip magna, you should always leave an authorpost even if you have the info box, because comments get clicks on recently created)
